name: Aeronautical Engineering
description: Aeronautical engineering focuses on the design, development, testing, and maintenance of aircraft and atmospheric flight vehicles. It encompasses aerodynamics, propulsion, structures, and control systems for airplanes, helicopters, and unmanned aerial vehicles operating within Earth's atmosphere.

subjects.name: Aerodynamics
subjects.description: Fundamental principles of fluid flow around solid objects with emphasis on aircraft applications. Covers incompressible and compressible flow, airfoil theory, boundary layers, lift and drag generation, and high-lift devices.
learning_outcomes: Apply potential flow theory and panel methods to predict aerodynamic forces on airfoils
Analyze boundary layer transition, separation, and its effect on drag
Calculate lift and drag characteristics of wings using lifting line theory
Evaluate compressibility effects in subsonic and transonic flows
subjects.industry_relevance: Core to aircraft design, performance analysis, and wind tunnel testing in aerospace companies.

subjects.name: Aircraft Structures
subjects.description: Analysis and design of aircraft structural components including wings, fuselage, and empennage. Covers stress-strain analysis, buckling, fatigue, composite materials, and airframe structural integrity under static and dynamic loads.
learning_outcomes: Perform stress and deflection analysis on aircraft beam and shell structures
Analyze buckling behavior of thin-walled aircraft structural components
Apply fatigue analysis methods for predicting airframe service life
Design composite laminates for aircraft structural applications
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for structural certification, airframe design, and maintenance in the aerospace industry.

subjects.name: Propulsion Systems
subjects.description: Study of aircraft propulsion including reciprocating engines, gas turbine engines (turbojet, turbofan, turboprop), and rocket engines. Covers thermodynamics of propulsion, combustion, nozzle theory, and engine performance analysis.
learning_outcomes: Analyze gas turbine engine cycles and calculate thrust and specific fuel consumption
Design compressor and turbine blade profiles for axial flow engines
Evaluate ramjet and scramjet performance for high-speed flight applications
Apply combustion fundamentals to design efficient and low-emission combustors
subjects.industry_relevance: Core to careers in aircraft engine manufacturing, airline maintenance, and propulsion R&D.

subjects.name: Flight Mechanics
subjects.description: Study of aircraft motion including performance, stability, and control. Covers equations of motion, longitudinal and lateral stability, stick-fixed and stick-free stability, control surface design, and handling qualities.
learning_outcomes: Calculate aircraft performance parameters including takeoff, climb, cruise, and landing distances
Analyze longitudinal and lateral-directional static stability of aircraft
Determine aircraft dynamic stability modes and their characteristics
Evaluate handling qualities using Cooper-Harper ratings and military specifications
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for flight test engineering, aircraft certification, and autopilot system design.

subjects.name: Aircraft Design
subjects.description: Conceptual and preliminary design of fixed-wing aircraft including requirements definition, configuration layout, weight estimation, performance analysis, and design optimization. Culminates in a comprehensive aircraft design project.
learning_outcomes: Develop aircraft design requirements from mission specifications
Perform weight estimation and sizing using statistical and analytical methods
Optimize wing, fuselage, and empennage configurations for mission performance
Prepare comprehensive aircraft design documentation and presentations
subjects.industry_relevance: Culminating design subject that prepares students for aircraft design roles in aerospace companies.

subjects.name: Gas Dynamics
subjects.description: Study of compressible fluid flow including normal and oblique shock waves, Prandtl-Meyer expansion fans, nozzle flow, and supersonic aerodynamics. Covers quasi-one-dimensional flow, method of characteristics, and wind tunnel design.
learning_outcomes: Analyze normal and oblique shock waves and calculate flow properties across them
Design converging-diverging nozzles for optimal supersonic expansion
Apply the method of characteristics for steady supersonic flow analysis
Evaluate supersonic wind tunnel operation and measurement techniques
subjects.industry_relevance: Fundamental for high-speed aircraft, missile aerodynamics, and rocket nozzle design.

subjects.name: Aircraft Materials
subjects.description: Materials used in aircraft construction including aluminum alloys, titanium, composites, and superalloys. Covers material selection, mechanical properties, corrosion, non-destructive testing, and advanced manufacturing processes for aerospace components.
learning_outcomes: Select appropriate materials for aircraft components based on strength, weight, and environmental factors
Apply non-destructive testing methods including ultrasonic, X-ray, and eddy current inspection
Analyze corrosion mechanisms and prevention strategies in aircraft structures
Evaluate composite manufacturing processes including autoclave curing and filament winding
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for aircraft manufacturing, maintenance repair and overhaul, and materials engineering roles.

subjects.name: Aircraft Maintenance
subjects.description: Aircraft maintenance practices including scheduled maintenance programs, trouble shooting, repair procedures, and airworthiness regulations. Covers aircraft systems inspection, overhaul procedures, and maintenance engineering management.
learning_outcomes: Develop scheduled maintenance programs compliant with regulatory requirements
Apply troubleshooting methodologies for aircraft system faults
Perform structural repair design and damage assessment for aircraft
Understand airworthiness certification and continuing airworthiness management
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for airline maintenance departments and MRO organizations ensuring flight safety.

subjects.name: Wind Tunnel Testing
subjects.description: Experimental aerodynamics in wind tunnels including test planning, model design, instrumentation, data acquisition, and correction methods. Covers subsonic, transonic, and supersonic wind tunnel techniques and flow visualization.
learning_outcomes: Design wind tunnel models considering scale effects and blockage corrections
Apply instrumentation including pressure sensors, force balances, and hot-wire anemometry
Perform flow visualization using smoke, tufts, and schlieren photography
Analyze and correct wind tunnel data for wall interference and support system effects
subjects.industry_relevance: Hands-on skills essential for aerodynamic testing roles in aerospace R&D and certification.

subjects.name: Avionics
subjects.description: Electronic systems in aircraft including communication, navigation, flight control, and cockpit instrumentation. Covers radar, GPS, ILS, autopilot systems, flight management computers, and modern glass cockpit technologies.
learning_outcomes: Understand aircraft communication systems including VHF, HF, and satcom
Analyze navigation systems including VOR, DME, ILS, GPS, and inertial navigation
Design autopilot control laws for pitch, roll, and yaw stabilization
Evaluate integrated avionics architectures including ARINC 429 and AFDX data buses
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for avionics design, aircraft modification, and airline engineering departments.

subjects.name: Flight Dynamics & Control
subjects.description: Advanced study of aircraft dynamics and automatic flight control systems including longitudinal and lateral equations of motion, transfer functions, stability augmentation systems, and autopilot design. Covers handling qualities and fly-by-wire systems.
learning_outcomes: Develop linearized equations of motion for aircraft dynamics analysis
Design stability augmentation systems for improved handling qualities
Implement autopilot modes including altitude hold, heading hold, and autoland
Evaluate fly-by-wire control laws and modern flight control architectures
subjects.industry_relevance: Core competency for flight control design in civil and military aircraft programs.

subjects.name: Aerospace Propulsion
subjects.description: Advanced propulsion concepts including ramjets, scramjets, rocket propulsion, and electric propulsion. Covers solid and liquid rocket motors, thrust chamber design, propellant chemistry, and performance analysis of advanced propulsion systems.
learning_outcomes: Analyze solid and liquid rocket motor performance using interior ballistics
Design ramjet and scramjet combustors for supersonic combustion
Evaluate electric propulsion systems including ion thrusters and Hall effect thrusters
Calculate rocket trajectories and stage optimization using Tsiolkovsky's rocket equation
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for careers in space launch, missile systems, and hypersonic vehicle development.

subjects.name: Aircraft Systems
subjects.description: Comprehensive study of aircraft subsystems including hydraulic, pneumatic, electrical, fuel, environmental control, landing gear, and de-icing systems. Covers system integration, redundancy, and safety in modern aircraft design.
learning_outcomes: Design hydraulic and pneumatic systems for aircraft actuation and braking
Analyze electrical power generation and distribution systems in modern aircraft
Evaluate environmental control systems for cabin pressurization and thermal management
Apply system safety analysis including FMEA and fault tree analysis to aircraft subsystems
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for aircraft systems engineering, integration, and certification roles.

subjects.name: Structural Analysis
subjects.description: Advanced structural analysis methods including finite element analysis for aerospace structures. Covers energy methods, matrix structural analysis, FEA theory, and practical application of commercial FEA software for aircraft component analysis.
learning_outcomes: Apply energy methods and matrix structural analysis for determinate and indeterminate structures
Develop finite element models using bar, beam, plate, and solid elements
Perform linear static and modal analysis of aircraft structural components
Validate FEA results through analytical solutions and experimental correlation
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential skill for stress analysis and structural certification in aerospace companies.

subjects.name: Computational Fluid Dynamics
subjects.description: Numerical methods for solving fluid flow problems including finite volume method, turbulence modeling, mesh generation, and CFD simulation of aerodynamic flows. Covers RANS, LES, and DNS approaches for aerospace applications.
learning_outcomes: Implement finite volume discretization of Navier-Stokes equations
Apply turbulence models including k-epsilon, k-omega, and Spalart-Allmaras
Generate structured and unstructured meshes for complex aerodynamic geometries
Validate CFD results against experimental data and analytical solutions
subjects.industry_relevance: Extensively used in aircraft aerodynamic design, reducing reliance on expensive wind tunnel testing.
